seoTitle: Start Page Numbers on a Specific Page in Google Docs
metaDescription: Skip your title page and start page numbering wherever your content begins in Google Docs, using section breaks and the Link to Previous setting.
intro: If you don't want your title page or introduction counted in your page numbers, Google Docs can handle that with section breaks. This guide covers isolating your intro pages, unlinking the header, and setting a custom starting number so your document paginates correctly.

steps.heading: Identify where numbering should start
steps.body: Decide which page marks the beginning of your core content, separate from any title or intro pages.

steps.heading: Insert a Section Break (Next Page)
steps.body: Use the Insert menu's Break panel to add a Section Break that isolates your intro pages from the rest of the document.

steps.heading: Open the target page's header or footer
steps.body: Navigate into the footer of your first numbered page to access its section-specific properties.

steps.heading: Turn off Link to Previous
steps.body: Disable the Link to Previous setting so this section's numbering is independent from earlier pages.

steps.heading: Set the starting number
steps.body: Use the Page Numbers wizard to insert numbering and set the starting index, then check the first-page checkbox to hide numbers on a cover page.

faq.question: How do I skip my title page in the page count?
faq.answer: Insert a Section Break (Next Page) before your first numbered page, then disable Link to Previous so numbering starts fresh.

faq.question: Can I make my content start at page 1 even if it's technically page 3?
faq.answer: Yes, the Page Numbers wizard lets you override the starting index so your content begins numbering at 1.

faq.question: Why do my new numbers still match the earlier section?
faq.answer: This usually means Link to Previous is still enabled, which keeps the sections tied together.

faq.question: Can I hide the number on my cover page only?
faq.answer: Yes, there's a checkbox option for a unique first page that hides numbering just on the cover.
